title: carex ochrochlamys
intro: Carex ochrochlamys, also known as yellow sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese and Japanese cultures, for its medicinal properties. The rhizomes of Carex ochrochlamys contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, offering health benefits such as reduced blood pressure and improved cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Carex ochrochlamys can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems.
